evidence: Task payload consumers still read both canonical contract fields and multiple legacy param paths: `src/shared/components/TaskDetails/VideoTravelDetails.tsx` (advanced_mode/motion_mode/phase/video refs/model fallbacks at lines 94-99, 156-163, 205-208), `supabase/functions/complete_task/generation.ts` (child/parent/order fallbacks at lines 112-124), `supabase/functions/complete_task/orchestrator.ts` (legacy run_id/orchestrator fallback queries at lines 290-377, 406-431), and `supabase/functions/calculate-task-cost/index.ts` (legacy sub-task fallback query at lines 313-334).

`src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClips.ts` still exposes a mixed-mode input interface with canonical + legacy + video-edit fields in one param bag (`JoinClipsTaskParams`, lines 90-159), then resolves mode via runtime branching (`resolveClipSource` lines 269-295, `resolveVideoEditConfig` lines 297-322, `normalizeJoinClipsParams` lines 330-389).

Lightbox/gallery composition still relies on very large prop tunnels and orchestration wrappers: `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/components/layouts/LightboxLayout.tsx` forwards extensive grouped props into `WorkflowControlsBar`/`WorkflowControls` (lines 241-270, 466-491), while `src/shared/components/MediaGallery/index.tsx` assembles and passes broad grouped objects (`itemShotWorkflow`, `itemActions`, lightbox props at lines 520-620).
abstraction_fitness.issues_preventing_higher_score: Canonical contract migration is incomplete across task-read paths and UI boundaries still depend on oversized prop bags/runtime branching, so abstraction layers do not yet enforce a single honest interface.

evidence: In `supabase/functions/complete_task/orchestrator.ts`, DB state-update failures throw (`markOrchestratorComplete` update at lines 515-527), but billing-path failures in the same flow are downgraded to log-only (`billingPersistError` at lines 555-560, `!billingResult.ok` at lines 562-567).

The billing service (`supabase/functions/calculate-task-cost/index.ts`) returns structured recoverable/failure signals (for example `subtask_query_failed` and `ledger_write_failed` responses), but orchestrator completion does not enforce a deterministic failure policy from those signals.
Current behavior preserves observability via `billing_outcome`, but persistence of that metadata itself is not guaranteed because write failure is only logged and completion still returns success.
error_consistency.issues_preventing_higher_score: Completion and billing still lack a single enforced failure policy; critical side effects can fail without changing orchestration outcome state.

findings.summary: Task-family readers still implement contract-plus-legacy fallback ladders instead of a single canonical payload read path.
evidence: `VideoTravelDetails` resolves the same semantics from `individualSegmentParams`, `orchestratorDetails`, and `parsedParams` (lines 94-99, 156-163, 205-208).
`generation.ts` still resolves lineage/order from both contract and legacy fields (`child_generation_id`, `parent_generation_id`, `segment_index`, `join_index`) at lines 112-124.
`orchestrator.ts` and `calculate-task-cost` both execute explicit legacy fallback DB filters when canonical queries return empty (orchestrator lines 290-377 and 406-431; billing lines 313-334).
findings.suggestion: Introduce one shared payload read adapter used by UI and edge functions (contract-only outputs with explicit migration state), then route all legacy reads through that adapter and remove ad-hoc per-module fallback chains once migration telemetry reaches zero.

findings.summary: Join-clips input contract still exposes legacy and modern modes in one oversized interface, keeping runtime branching in core task creation.
evidence: `JoinClipsTaskParams` includes canonical descriptors (`mode`, `clip_source`, `video_edit`) plus legacy top-level fields (`starting_video_path`, `video_edit_mode`, `source_video_*`, etc.) in one type (lines 90-159).

Task creation still infers mode/source at runtime (`resolveClipSource` and `resolveVideoEditConfig`) before validating mode consistency in `normalizeJoinClipsParams` (lines 269-389), indicating call sites are not constrained to explicit mode-specific contracts.
findings.suggestion: Split creation entry points into explicit mode contracts (e.g., `createMultiClipJoin`, `createLegacyJoinCompat`, `createVideoEditJoin`) and keep one small legacy adapter at the boundary that normalizes to strict internal DTOs.

findings.summary: Lightbox/gallery orchestration relies on broad prop-bag tunneling, increasing indirection and weakening ownership boundaries.
evidence: `LightboxLayout` forwards many fields from nested grouped props to multiple downstream controls (`WorkflowControlsBar` lines 241-270 and `WorkflowControls` lines 466-491).
`MediaGallery` builds and passes several large grouped objects (`itemShotWorkflow`, `itemMobileInteraction`, `itemActions`, plus large lightbox prop sets) at lines 520-620.
`useVideoLightboxRenderModel`/`ImageLightbox` construct expansive environment/state models and pass through broad slices, creating wrapper layers with low policy density.
findings.suggestion: Extract explicit feature-level controller interfaces (shot actions, variant actions, segment navigation) and let leaf components consume context/selectors directly instead of passing full orchestration bags through layout components.

findings.summary: The deprecated Supabase legacy facade remains an active exported abstraction layer, preserving dual client surfaces.
evidence: `src/integrations/supabase/legacy/index.ts` is still a public re-export surface (`export { supabase, getLegacySupabaseClient }` at line 10) despite deprecation metadata.

Parallel active usage of the modern client API exists in hooks (`useTasks` line 3 and `useGenerationMutations` line 19 import `getSupabaseClient`), confirming dual abstraction surfaces remain available.
findings.suggestion: Convert legacy facade to an internal-only migration shim (or hard-fail in non-allowlisted importers), then enforce a single client entry point and track remaining callers with a shrinking allowlist.

findings.summary: Orchestrator completion mixes fatal and log-only failure handling for billing in one control path.
evidence: `markOrchestratorComplete` throws on orchestrator task update failure (`updateOrchError`), but does not throw when `triggerCostCalculation` returns `ok: false`.
When persisting `billing_outcome` fails (`billingPersistError`), code only logs and continues, despite this metadata being the reconciliation artifact for non-success billing outcomes.
`calculate-task-cost` emits structured error codes/recoverability, but no shared policy maps those to required orchestrator state transitions or retry scheduling.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a shared billing completion policy helper used by orchestrator completion: map billing result classes (`recorded`, `skipped`, `recoverable_failure`, `nonrecoverable_failure`, `persistence_failure`) to explicit actions (complete-with-retry-flag, fail-task, or enqueue compensating retry), and enforce that policy before returning.

findings.summary: Failure to persist billing outcome metadata is logged but not propagated or compensated.
evidence: After billing call, `billing_outcome` is written to `tasks.result_data`; on write failure (`billingPersistError`) only `console.error` is emitted and function continues.
If billing call itself fails, orchestrator logs and exits without guaranteed durable retry marker beyond best-effort metadata write.
Because `calculate-task-cost` returns machine-readable error metadata, dropping persistence removes the primary durable context needed for later reconciliation.
findings.suggestion: Make billing-outcome persistence part of a required post-completion contract: if metadata write fails, either mark task with a dedicated reconciliation-needed state via fallback update path or raise a handled error that triggers retry workflow.
